notes.anchor: fourth day before the Ides of May
notes.note: 10 May. The Roman dating system counts inclusively backwards from the Ides (the 15th in May).
L. Cornelius Lentulus Crus and C. Claudius Marcellus, consuls of 49 BC: the year of the civil war's outbreak. The Pompeian villa is Cicero's at Cumae.
Juba I of Numidia, ally of the Pompeians; after Pharsalus the African remnant under Cato, Scipio and Labienus regrouped at his court, where the war ended at Thapsus in April 46 BC.
Customary places of polite Roman exile in the Greek east; chosen here as a metaphorical floor of indignity, beneath which Cicero is determined not to be set.
